original_abstract: Measuring the degree of similarity between textual units is a fundamental component of various Natural Language Processing tasks, ranging from information retrieval and document clustering to machine translation and automatic essay grading. This paper provides a comprehensive survey of text similarity approaches, categorizing them into three primary frameworks: string-based, corpus-based, and knowledge-based measures. String-based metrics evaluate lexical similarity through character-level and term-level comparisons. Corpus-based approaches utilize statistical information derived from large-scale text collections to capture semantic resonance, while knowledge-based methods leverage organized semantic networks, such as WordNet, to quantify relationships between concepts. In addition to individual methodologies, the survey examines hybrid models that integrate multiple techniques to achieve higher accuracy. By consolidating the most prominent algorithms and existing software packages, this survey serves as a technical roadmap for researchers and practitioners working on semantic and lexical text analysis.
humanized_abstract: Text similarity measurement is one of the common tasks in Natural Language Processing (NLP). It is applied in many applications including search engines, text classification, machine translation and automatic essay grading. This survey reviews the text similarity measurements techniques. It mainly focuses on three measurement approaches: string-based, corpus-based and knowledge-based measurements. In the string-based measurements, the techniques used are the matching at the character level and word level. While in the corpus-based measurements, it makes use of the statistical information present in the corpora to evaluate the degree of semantic relatedness between texts. And in the knowledge-based measurements, it refers to using the semantic relationship present in a knowledge base such as WordNet. The survey also reviews the hybrid models that combine various measurements to enhance the measurements performance. It aims at providing a technical reference for the researches and applications in the field of semantic and lexical analysis of texts.
